title: Liable To Originate (3rd)
text:
## 13. Ignorance

At one time Venerable Sāriputta and Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita were staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana. … Mahākoṭṭhita said to Sāriputta: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘knowledge’. What is knowledge? And how is a knowing one defined?”

“Reverend, it’s when a learned noble disciple truly understands form, which is liable to originate … liable to fall … liable to originate and fall, as form which is liable to originate and fall. They truly understand feeling … perception … choices … consciousness, which is liable to originate … liable to fall … liable to originate and fall, as consciousness which is liable to originate and fall. This is called knowledge. And this is how a knowing one is defined.”
